title: carex rostrata subsp. hymenocarpa
intro: Carex rostrata subsp. hymenocarpa, also known as the Northern or Sedge, is a subspecies of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae and is native to North America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory conditions, and has been used to treat conditions like bronchitis and coughs. Carex rostrata subsp. hymenocarpa is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in topical applications for skin irritations.
